35798549.1620.comment: Given his excellent pedigree/connections, SHIPBOURNE very much looks the type to improve on handicap debut. He's narrowly preferred to Asmen Warrior, whose second on penultimate start at Windsor has been well-advertised. Newmarket specialist Spanish Voice is another to note in a competitive affair, along with progressive 3-y-os The Joker and Lion of Alba.

runners.comment: Quirky/useful handicapper in 2024. Last seen finishing well beaten in an all-weather listed race on final start for Marco Botti in November of that year. Entitled to need this stable debut following 20-month absence.

Back on the up when successful under a more-patient ride over C&D last time (now 3-3 at Newmarket, winning twice on Rowley Mile). Plenty of pace on so should get race to suit again (provided same tactics are adopted), and he makes plenty of appeal with Ryan Moore taking over.
Probably in better heart than this season's form figures would suggest. Caught too far back in the Buckingham Palace latest. Best form has come at Ascot, though (won Victoria Cup there last year).

Low-mileage 4-y-o who was narrowly touched off in this race 12 months ago. Only 1 lb higher in bid to go one better, and he looked right at the top of his game behind a course specialist at Ripon in late-May.

One-time smart performer who finished close third off 7 lb higher at Sandown (1¼m) this time last year. Went backwards from his reappearance in higher grade on all-weather latest, underlining he's more miss than hit nowadays.

Well beaten both starts since winning comfortably over the Rowley Mile (1m) in May. Still 1 lb higher than when winning this race in 2024 and likely to be ridden to pick up the pieces under Jamie Spencer.

Proved a different proposition when pulling clear with an improving, next-time-out winner at Windsor in late-May. Visor he wore that day back on (didn't fully settle in blinkers in a messy Epsom handicap latest). Well worth another chance to confirm Windsor form.

Relatively lightly-raced 4-y-o has won twice since joining current yard. Lacked the pace to land a blow when sixth in a slightly better race at Sandown last time. Strongly-run 1m on this course may just suit.
Progressive sort. Landed a second double of his career when successful in the Carlisle Bell last time. Hat-trick likely to prove beyond him off career-high mark in better company.

Capped progressive juvenile season with all-the-way success at Chester last August. No impact in Italian 2000 Guineas on return, but he made the most of an advantageous draw when fourth in the Britannia on handicap bow. Still unexposed and Oisin Murphy aboard for first time.

Successful 4 times on the all-weather last season. Chased home Spanish Voice and Sterling Knight in handicaps on Rowley Mile here in May. Looked unlucky not to finish second in really competitive affair at Epsom, and he may again benefit from a strong pace to aim at.

Well-bred son of Frankel. Off since opening his account in small-field novice at Wolverhampton in November 2024. Sold out of Roger Varian for 40,000 gns in February this year (gelded in interim). Capable new yard pitch him into the deep end on belated return/handicap debut.

Bred in the purple. Impressive debut winner at Newbury (6f, heavy) last October. Built on that at the second attempt when winning a Doncaster novice (7f) on reappearance. There may well be better to come now sent handicapping over longer trip by excellent yard (smart brother Task Force stayed 9f).
Best run last year came when third in C&D handicap. Since extended long losing run on turf with creditable fourth over 7f here on his return and now steps back up in grade/trip.

Steadily progressive sort who justified support with a smooth-travelling success at Sandown (9f). A 3 lb rise looks more than fair (always holding on last time), and he could run another big race with luck in running from stall 15.

35801923.1250.comment: None of these can be ruled out but the vote goes to PRINCLING who can race off the same mark as when a very good fifth in the Golden Gates at Royal Ascot when doing best of those held up. Laureate Crown also shaped better than his position suggests at Royal Ascot when mid-field in the Britannia and could emerge as the main danger.

runners.comment: Very useful effort when winning handicap on Rowley course here (1¼m) in May and backed it up with a good seventh of 16 in Golden Gates at Royal Ascot last month. Enthusiastic type must enter calculations.

Made a winning reappearance at Wolverhampton (8.5f) and improved again to follow up in Esher Cup at Sandown (1m) in April. Shaped well when twelfth of 30 in Britannia at Royal Ascot since, first home in his group. Same mark and he remains well ahead of it on that showing.

Won his first 2 starts after joining Marco Botti, including on Rowley course here (1¼m) in April. Not at his best when ninth of 16 in Italian Derby at Milan last time but no surprise to see him bounce back here.

Improving Study of Man colt who landed 1m novice at Windsor in May and followed up in similar event at Nottingham (1m) 29 days ago, despite still looking green in front. Capable of better still now going handicapping over this longer trip.

Promising Kingman colt who landed 1m Wetherby novice on reappearance before producing useful efforts when fourth in Silver Bowl at Carlisle (1m) and fifth in Golden Gates at Royal Ascot (1¼m). Fared best of those held up on latter occasion so he merits serious consideration.

Kingman colt who built on promising debut to win 1m Kempton maiden on return. Improved again when second of 11 to Decade of Time in novice at Windsor (1m) in May when caught further back than ideal. More to offer now going up in trip for handicap debut.
